title: Add pod start and stop
body:
As well as added tests, man pages, and completions.
Also reformat and refactor a couple of other small things in the other pod commands.

Signed-off-by: haircommander <pehunt@redhat.com>

comments.body: I might take away "else" from your laptop.  :^)
Oh dear, the tests are actually green.

At some point we should consider allowing the IDs of individual containers that were stopped/started to be printed during the output, maybe like so:

podman start abcde
abced
- ctr1id
- ctr2id
- ctr3id

But that would require extra work on the libpod side to expose the state changes that happened.

LGTM, this is a good start.

SGTM, the change would be effectively trivial on the podman side. I wonder if a -q should supress it, and have it print ctr ids by default? If we go forward with that we should also add it to rm, and eventually kill/pause/unpause...

I think we'd have a `-q` which would print just pod IDs, and maybe a `-v` for verbose that would print container IDs as well? We could even get fancy and add a `-vv` for double-verbose that would print the IDs of both containers that started and containers that were already started.

@mheon then what would the default be? I feel like:
-q for pod ID,
nothing for pod and ctr-id
-v for pod and ctr-id along with those already started
if the latter would be useful.
That sounds like a good way of doing it, yeah

I'll focus on the rest of the pod commands first, but I can look into that after.

Speaking of which @rhatdan @umohnani8 @TomSweeneyRedHat  PTAL it'd be cool to get this in, it'll make testing the next commands easier
I'm going to merge this so we can cut a release. Namespaces will wait until Monday.
I'd actually like to add name spaces to pod create and ps anyway so I think it makes sense to wait
Should just work on your side - all the magic happens in the database.
at least with ps I need to manually include it in the output.
I don't think it's worth including in `ps` output - seems more like an `inspect` thing to me.
crictl includes it in their pods output, which is our ps. so I think @rhatdan wants it in for CRI-O
